text: check-restatement caught the ADR restating the rule mechanics from pre-registration.md on first draft — fixed by pointing at the home instead;

diagnosing the validate.py pass required discovering that the validator counts unicode codepoints while wc -c counts bytes (6,036 bytes = 5,992 codepoints) — a measure-the-right-unit trap for anyone using wc -c against the skill validator's budgets.
`python` is not on PATH in this WSL2 environment, only `python3` — validate.py invocation failed once.

the adr-lint.md spec addition pushed the reference past the 6,000-codepoint REFERENCE_TOC_THRESHOLD, caught by validate.py, trimmed after the fact — SECOND instance this session of editing a budgeted file without measuring first (first was ADR 0066 drafted 130 chars over the lite budget; the prior branch retrospective recorded that as instance one and said a second occurrence justifies a rung-1 poka-yoke, e.g. a measure-first line in the /decide skill's authoring step);

one audit finding revealed test-post-edit-gate.sh silently SKIPs everywhere but the owner's Windows machine (hard-coded path), meaning CI asserts nothing for that hook — documented in the header now, but the underlying CI gap remains unfixed.
check-restatement caught the amended ADR 0051 sharing a 14-word verbatim span with the reworded adr-template.md rule — fixed by rewording the ADR side (one home);
